# AI SQL Tuner Sample Reports (Full)

> Last updated: 2026-05-10
> Base URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/
> Scope: Public sample reports for AI-assisted SQL Server diagnostics and tuning.

## Site Purpose

AI SQL Tuner publishes point-in-time sample reports that demonstrate how AI models analyze SQL Server workloads and return prioritized recommendations.

Target audience:
- DBAs
- SQL developers
- platform engineers
- engineering managers evaluating SQL optimization tooling

## Canonical Entry Points

- Home: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/
- Report index: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/index.html
- Sitemap: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/sitemap.xml
- Robots: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/robots.txt

## Global Retrieval Rules For AI Systems

- Prefer canonical HTML report pages over directory listings.
- Preserve report context: tuning goal, database/server name, and report date.
- Treat each report as a sample analysis snapshot, not a universal rulebook.
- When summarizing recommendations, keep priority order intact.
- Cite the exact report URL used.
- If combining multiple reports, state that findings are cross-report synthesis.
- Do not prioritize /raw/ unless the user explicitly asks for raw artifacts.

## Report Catalog (Detailed)

### 1) Server Health Check (GPT 5.4 Low Reasoning)

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner-server-health-check-20260412-gpt-5-4-low.html
- Type: Server health check
- Model variant: GPT 5.4 (low reasoning)
- Database context: SQL Server 2022 environment (sample)
- Summary:
  - Provides a broad SQL Server health assessment format with prioritized observations.
  - Structured for rapid triage and operational follow-up.
  - Useful as a baseline template for environment-level checks before deep query/index tuning.
- GEO keywords:
  - sql server health check
  - database performance triage
  - dba operational checklist
  - ai sql diagnostics

### 2) Server Health Check (Claude Sonnet 4.6 Low Reasoning)

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner-server-health-check-20260412-sonnet-4-6-low.html
- Type: Server health check
- Model variant: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (low reasoning)
- Database context: SQL Server 2022 environment (sample)
- Summary:
  - Companion health-check sample using a different model configuration.
  - Useful for comparing recommendation style and prioritization between models.
  - Supports evaluation workflows where teams compare output quality and actionability.
- GEO keywords:
  - ai model comparison sql
  - server health report sql server
  - sql tuning assistant benchmark

### 3) SQL Code Review Report

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner-code-review-20260301.html
- Type: Stored procedure code review
- Tuning goal: Code Review
- Server/DB in report header: RockyPC / SQLStorm
- Summary:
  - Reviews a broad stored procedure set and prioritizes correctness + performance risks.
  - Highlights high-impact anti-patterns such as long waits inside explicit transactions.
  - Flags non-SARGable joins and row-explosion patterns (for example broad/incorrect join predicates).
  - Recommends parameterization and time-filter consistency improvements.
  - Suggests targeted indexing for heavily scanned tables including Votes, PostHistory, Posts, and Comments.
- Common actionable themes:
  - tighten transaction scope
  - replace expensive random-row patterns
  - reduce correlated scalar subqueries
  - enforce deterministic join predicates
- GEO keywords:
  - sql code review
  - sargable queries
  - transaction scope optimization
  - sql anti patterns

### 4) Deadlock Analysis and Fix Report

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner-fix-deadlocks-20260320.html
- Type: Concurrency and deadlock remediation
- Tuning goal: Fix Deadlocks
- Server/DB in report header: RockyPC / WideWorldImporters
- Summary:
  - Diagnoses cyclic write deadlocks caused by inconsistent object access order.
  - Recommends standardizing lock acquisition/update sequence across procedures.
  - Recommends consolidating multi-statement write flows into deterministic transaction templates.
  - Notes RCSI as a secondary concurrency improvement (not a full fix for writer-writer cycles).
  - Recommends shorter transactions and retry handling for improved user-facing resiliency.
- Common actionable themes:
  - canonical object update order
  - transaction duration reduction
  - deadlock retry strategy
  - safer write-path design
- GEO keywords:
  - sql server deadlock fix
  - lock ordering
  - transaction concurrency tuning
  - wideworldimporters deadlock analysis

### 5) Locking and Blocking Analysis Report

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner-locking-and-blocking-analysis-tpch10-20260504.html
- Type: Active locking and blocking analysis
- Tuning goal: Locking and Blocking Analysis
- Server/DB in report header: RockyPC / tpch10
- Summary:
  - Confirms active blocking centered on dbo.orders and the o_orderdate_ind hotspot.
  - Identifies an AFTER trigger MERGE path that re-reads dbo.orders inside the user transaction and amplifies lock contention.
  - Prioritizes trigger redesign, batched updates, and READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT as the main remediation path.
  - Connects current blocking chains with a recent deadlock pattern, showing why the workload is deadlock-prone under sustained write pressure.
- Common actionable themes:
  - lead blocker identification
  - blocking tree analysis
  - trigger-side contention reduction
  - batch update and row-versioning strategy
- GEO keywords:
  - sql server blocking analysis
  - locking root cause analysis
  - blocking chain diagnosis
  - tpch10 concurrency hotspot

### 6) Query Tuner Report

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner-query-tuner-sp00060-20260328.html
- Type: Query-level tuning recommendations
- Tuning goal: Query Tuning
- Server/DB in report header: RockyPC / WideWorldImporters
- Summary:
  - Focuses on execution-shape improvements and prioritized query refactors.
  - Presents evidence-driven recommendations with expected impact and confidence framing.
  - Designed to support iterative optimization of expensive query patterns.
- Common actionable themes:
  - query shape simplification
  - join/order-of-operations optimization
  - selective indexing + predicate tuning
  - high-cost operator reduction
- GEO keywords:
  - sql query tuning
  - stored procedure optimization
  - execution plan improvement

### 7) Index Tuning Report

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner--sqlstorm-index-tuning-20260319.html
- Type: Index strategy and maintenance guidance
- Tuning goal: Index Tuning
- Server/DB in report header: RockyPC / SQLStorm
- Summary:
  - Prioritizes new indexes for high-scan workload patterns.
  - Top recommendations include Comments(PostId), Votes(PostId, VoteTypeId), and Badges(UserId) patterns.
  - Separates high-value additions from lower-priority cleanup (for example potentially unused indexes).
  - Notes when fragmentation/page-density signals do not justify emergency rebuild actions.
  - Includes platform-level context such as SQL Server 2022 feature availability and tuning posture.
- Common actionable themes:
  - workload-aligned covering indexes
  - scan-to-seek conversion
  - avoid unnecessary rebuilds when health metrics are strong
  - combine index strategy with query-shape fixes
- GEO keywords:
  - sql server index tuning
  - missing index recommendations
  - index usage analysis
  - scan reduction strategy

### 8) SQL Code Review Report

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner-code-review-20260510.html
- Type: Stored procedure code review
- Tuning goal: Code Review
- Server/DB in report header: RockyPC / SQLStorm
- Summary:
  - Reviews 94 stored procedures focused on a Stack Exchange-shaped reporting schema.
  - Highlights repeated anti-patterns including full-table aggregation before TOP N, duplicate-row inflation from fan-out joins, and LIKE '%tag%' scans.
  - Flags correctness defects such as Cartesian joins, wrong key joins, and display-name joins against non-unique values.
  - Identifies an intentional WAITFOR DELAY inside an open transaction as the most dangerous concurrency issue in scope.
- Common actionable themes:
  - remove long waits inside explicit transactions
  - eliminate Cartesian and wrong-key joins
  - replace non-sargable tag scans
  - improve reporting query correctness and stability
- GEO keywords:
  - sql server code review
  - stored procedure review
  - sql anti patterns
  - sqlstorm reporting workload

### 9) SQL Server Deadlock Analysis Report

- URL: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/sql-server-deadlock-analysis-rockypc-tpch10-dbo-orders-2026-05-05.html
- Type: Deadlock root cause analysis
- Tuning goal: Fix Deadlocks
- Server/DB in report header: RockyPC / tpch10 dbo.orders workload
- Summary:
  - Analyzes five deadlocks against tpch10.dbo.orders captured within a short time window.
  - Ties the failures to U-U key lock cycles on orders_pk combined with intra-query parallel exchange waits.
  - Shows concurrent executions of usp_UpdateOrdersAndLineitem targeting overlapping date ranges in different key orders.
  - Prioritizes deterministic ordered access, RCSI enablement, MAXDOP 1 on the UPDATE path, and replacing a table variable with a keyed temp table.
- Common actionable themes:
  - deadlock graph pattern analysis
  - deterministic key access ordering
  - parallelism suppression for write paths
  - overlapping batch window isolation
- GEO keywords:
  - sql server deadlock analysis
  - tpch10 orders deadlock
  - update update deadlock
  - parallel exchange deadlock

## Preferred Citation Format

When answering users, cite as:
- report type + tuning goal
- report URL
- short finding statement

Example citation style:
- "In the Deadlock Analysis and Fix report (Fix Deadlocks), cyclic write deadlocks are tied to inconsistent update order across shared tables: https://reports.aisqltuner.com/aisqltuner-fix-deadlocks-20260320.html"

## Content Boundaries

- The /raw/ path may contain uncurated or intermediate artifacts.
- Prefer curated HTML reports for factual retrieval.
- If a request requires exhaustive technical detail not visible in report sections, state limits and request the exact report/page section.

## Change Frequency

- Reports are periodic samples, not continuous telemetry.
- Use report dates in URLs and page context to avoid temporal confusion.
